title: Editor’s picks · the weekend of July 17–19

events.title: Take a Spin at LeMay
events.loc: LeMay – America’s Car Museum · 2702 E. D St., Tacoma
events.desc: Ride along in a classic car from the museum’s collection on a spin around Haub Family Field, then make a day of the exhibits and grab lunch at the new café.

events.title: Fircrest Fun Days
events.loc: Fircrest Park · 555 Contra Costa Ave., Fircrest
events.desc: An Old West–themed festival with live concerts, artist and vendor booths, food trucks, bake-offs, a Kidz Zone with story time and face painting — and Saturday fireworks.

events.title: GeekFest West
events.desc: Snohomish County’s biggest geek gathering: cosplay, LARPing, chess, video games and pinball, with Sunday geared to families — kids’ cosplay demos, tournaments and tutorials.

events.title: Kla Ha Ya Days
events.loc: Historic Downtown Snohomish · 1024 First St., Snohomish
events.desc: A century-old summer tradition in downtown Snohomish: a kids fair and outdoor movie Friday, pancake breakfast, street fair, parade and circus acts Saturday, and a Sunday car show.

events.title: Family Fun Day at Highline SeaTac Botanical Garden
events.loc: Highline SeaTac Botanical Garden · 13735 24th Ave. S., SeaTac
events.desc: Stilt walkers, live music, garden yoga, demonstrations and pollinator lessons make this garden festival an easy, free win with kids.

events.title: Lavender Festival
events.loc: JB Family Growers · 14063 N.E. 145th St., Woodinville
events.desc: Fields of 15,000 lavender plants in full bloom (with sunflowers starting too) — walk the rows, sip lavender lemonade and shop local vendors.

events.title: SandBlast Festival of the Arts
events.loc: McCormick Park · 26200 N.E. Stephens St., Duvall
events.desc: Watch artists carve remarkable sand sculptures on the riverside beach over two days, with local artwork and live music alongside.

events.title: Downtown Seattle Block Party
events.desc: A kickoff party for the Nordstrom Anniversary Sale with kid-friendly activities, games and live entertainment — plus appearances by the Mariner Moose and Blitz the Seahawk.

events.title: Seattle Bon Odori Festival
events.loc: Seattle Betsuin Buddhist Temple · 1427 S. Main St., Seattle
events.desc: Taiko drumming, Japanese folk dancing, festival foods (shaved ice!), a thrift store and a beer garden at this beloved summer celebration honoring ancestral spirits.

events.title: Parade of Nations and Watch Party
events.loc: Rainier Playfield · 3700 S. Alaska St., Seattle
events.desc: Celebrate the World Cup final (kickoff at noon Sunday) with a parade celebrating diversity and unity, soccer-themed activities and food trucks.
